name: VWAP Reclaim
description: Price loses the session VWAP intraday, then reclaims it with rising relative volume — a mean-reversion/continuation setup to watch, common in retail futures-trading communities.
requiredContext: Price closes below session VWAP for at least 2 consecutive bars
Price closes back above VWAP on rising relative volume vs. the prior bar
confirmationSigns: Volume module reads 'confirms' rather than 'rejects' on the reclaim bar
Order flow bias not strongly opposed to the reclaim direction
invalidationSigns: Price closes back below VWAP before any target is reached
riskNotes: Historical/contextual bias only — size and stops are the trader's own responsibility. Works best with volume confirmation; weak without it.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Morning trend window (10:00–11:30 ET), after the opening range establishes a level to reclaim
avoidConditions: Within 15 minutes of a high-impact scheduled release

name: Liquidity Sweep Reversal
description: A fast wick through a well-known prior high/low (resting liquidity), followed by a quick reclaim back inside the prior range — a 'stop run then reverse' pattern to watch.
requiredContext: A clearly defined prior session high or low
A wick piercing that level, ideally on a volume spike
A close back inside the prior range
confirmationSigns: Order flow module flags a liquidity sweep or trapped-traders read
ICT module flags a liquidity sweep concept on the same symbol
invalidationSigns: Price closes back beyond the wick extreme (failed reclaim)
riskNotes: Conditions to monitor, not a guaranteed reversal — liquidity sweeps can fail outright in strongly trending conditions.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Any session with a clearly defined prior high/low nearby; most commonly watched around the opening range and NY AM window
avoidConditions: Strongly trending, event-driven days where sweeps tend to simply continue rather than reverse

name: Opening Range Breakout (ORB)
description: Price breaks outside the first 30 minutes' high/low range with conviction — a classic momentum setup to watch on trend days.
requiredContext: A defined opening range (09:30–10:00 ET)
A close beyond the range high or low
Supportive relative volume on the break
confirmationSigns: Rhythm module reads 'trending' for the session
Volume module reads 'confirms' on the breakout bar
invalidationSigns: Price re-enters the opening range shortly after breaking out (failed breakout)
riskNotes: Works best on genuine trend days; tends to produce failed breakouts on range/choppy days. Historical/contextual bias only.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Opening range (09:30–10:00 ET) through the morning trend window (10:00–11:30 ET)
avoidConditions: Rhythm module reads 'choppy' or 'ranging' for the session

name: Trend Continuation Pullback
description: A shallow pullback within an established intraday trend, watched for a resumption in the direction of the prevailing move.
requiredContext: An established directional trend on the session
A pullback that holds a prior structure point rather than breaking it
confirmationSigns: Order flow bias aligned with the prevailing trend direction
invalidationSigns: Pullback breaks the prior structure point that defined the trend
riskNotes: Requires confirmation that the trend is still intact — late-stage trends are more prone to exhaustion. Historical/contextual bias only.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Morning trend window (10:00–11:30 ET) or early afternoon (13:30–15:00 ET) once a trend is established

name: News Reaction Retest
description: After an initial volatile reaction to a high-importance headline or release, price pulls back to retest the pre-news level or the breakout level — watched as a second, lower-volatility entry opportunity.
requiredContext: A high-importance news impact card or high-impact calendar event today
An initial sharp directional reaction
A retest of the pre-news level or breakout level that holds
confirmationSigns: Retest holds without fully reversing the initial reaction
Volume cools on the retest vs. the initial spike
invalidationSigns: Price fully reverses through the pre-news level on the retest
riskNotes: Requires confirmation that the retest is holding — first reactions to news are often choppy and two-sided. Risk warning: avoid sizing up in the first few minutes after the headline.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Within 30–90 minutes after a high-importance headline or scheduled release
avoidConditions: Trading the first 1–2 minutes immediately after the headline, before any retest has formed

name: Gold / DXY / Yield Confirmation
description: Uses the typical inverse relationship between gold and the US Dollar (and the broader rates backdrop) as a cross-asset confirmation check before treating a gold setup as higher-quality context.
requiredContext: A directional setup on Gold (GC)
DXY reading in the opposite direction on the heat map module, consistent with the historical inverse relationship
confirmationSigns: Heat map correlation note confirms the typical Gold/USD inverse relationship is holding today
invalidationSigns: Heat map correlation note flags the Gold/USD relationship as NOT holding today — cross-asset confirmation is absent
riskNotes: This is a contextual confirmation check, not a standalone setup — use it to add or subtract confidence in a separately-identified Gold setup. Historical/contextual bias only; the relationship weakens periodically.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Any session, but most relevant around US data releases that move rates and the dollar together
avoidConditions: Heat map module flags the Gold/USD relationship as currently decoupled

name: Oil Inventory Reaction
description: Watches Crude Oil (CL) around the weekly EIA inventories release for a short-lived volatility spike and potential follow-through.
requiredContext: EIA Crude Oil Inventories on today's calendar
An initial directional reaction to the release (larger-than-expected draws are typically watched as bullish context; larger builds as bearish context)
confirmationSigns: Reaction holds or extends in the first 10–15 minutes after release
Volume module reads a spike on the release bar
invalidationSigns: Price round-trips back through the pre-release level within minutes
riskNotes: Reactions to this release are often sharp and short-lived; risk warning: spreads can widen quickly in the first minute. Historical/contextual bias only.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: Around the scheduled EIA release time (typically mid-morning ET)
avoidConditions: No EIA Crude Oil Inventories event on today's calendar — context for this strategy doesn't apply

name: Forex Session Breakout
description: Watches for a directional breakout as a major FX session opens (London or NY), when liquidity and participation step up.
requiredContext: Price coiling in a tight range into a session open (London or NY)
A break of that range shortly after the session opens, on rising participation
confirmationSigns: ICT module shows an active kill zone (London open or NY AM)
Order flow bias aligned with the breakout direction
invalidationSigns: Price re-enters the pre-breakout range shortly after the session open
riskNotes: Historical/contextual bias only — session-open breakouts can be two-sided before a real direction emerges. Risk warning: avoid oversizing the first move.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: London open kill zone (02:00–05:00 ET) or NY AM kill zone (07:00–10:00 ET)
avoidConditions: No active kill zone currently flagged by the ICT module

name: ICT Liquidity Sweep + Market Structure Shift
description: A two-step ICT-style sequence: a sweep of resting liquidity beyond a prior high/low, followed by a market structure shift in the opposite direction — watched together as stronger context than either alone.
requiredContext: ICT module flags a liquidity sweep concept
ICT module also flags a market structure shift concept in the direction opposite the swept side
Ideally inside an active kill zone
confirmationSigns: Both concepts point the same directional way (sweep down + bullish MSS, or sweep up + bearish MSS)
Active kill zone at the time of the sequence
invalidationSigns: Structure resumes the pre-sweep trend without confirming the shift
riskNotes: ICT-style concepts describe a popular price-action framework, not a guaranteed system — this is a setup to watch requiring confirmation, never a guaranteed trade.
bestSessionOrTimeWindow: NY AM kill zone (07:00–10:00 ET) or London open kill zone (02:00–05:00 ET)
avoidConditions: Only one of the two concepts (sweep or MSS) is flagged without the other
